summary: PRC Subscription Burn Rate (5%) Implementation VERIFIED. Backend returns burn_prc=784.62, burn_rate_percent=5, total_prc=16477.05. Frontend displays 'Burning (5%)' line with +784 PRC in breakdown. All useState(10) changed to useState(null), all admin-only PRC rate calls changed to public /api/prc-economy/current-rate.

GET /api/prc-economy/current-rate.details: Returns final_rate=11 (public endpoint, no auth required)

POST /api/subscription/pay-with-prc.details: Accepts ~16,477 PRC as valid amount (within 10% variance)

Frontend Burn Line Display.details: Shows 'Burning (5%)' with +784 PRC in PRC Calculation Breakdown

Frontend PRC Rate Display.details: Shows '11 PRC = ₹1' (Dynamic) - fetched from public endpoint

Frontend useState Changes.details: GiftVoucherRedemption.js, RedeemPageV2.js, DashboardModern.js all use useState(null) and /api/prc-economy/current-rate

critical_code_review_comments: calculate_elite_prc_price() correctly calculates burn as 5% of (base_prc + processing_fee_prc + admin_charges_prc)
Burn formula: subtotal_before_burn = 15,692.42 PRC, burn_prc = 5% × 15,692.42 = 784.62 PRC, total = 16,477.05 PRC
PRCRateDisplay component correctly shows Burning (5%) row when burnRate prop > 0
All frontend files now use useState(null) for prcRate instead of hardcoded useState(10)
All frontend files now use public /api/prc-economy/current-rate instead of admin-only endpoint
context_for_next_testing_agent: PRC subscription burn rate (5%) is now fully implemented. Backend calculate_elite_prc_price() includes burn_prc in total. Frontend PRCRateDisplay shows 'Burning (5%)' line. All hardcoded PRC rate useState(10) changed to useState(null). All admin-only PRC rate API calls changed to public /api/prc-economy/current-rate endpoint.

GET /api/subscription/elite-pricing.formula: ₹999 + 18% GST + ₹10 Processing Fee + 20% Admin Charges + 5% Burn

burn_rate_implementation.formula: Burn = 5% × (Base PRC + Processing PRC + Admin PRC)
